component.name: NV Broadcast
component.summary: AI-powered virtual camera with background removal and meeting transcription

description.p: NV Broadcast brings NVIDIA Broadcast features to Linux. AI-powered virtual camera with background removal, eye contact correction, face relighting, voice studio, and meeting transcription — all running locally on your GPU.

ul.li: Background blur, replace, or remove with 3 AI models
Eye contact correction — look at camera while reading your screen
Face relighting — fill light guided by the scene
Voice studio — bass, treble, warmth, compression with GPU acceleration
AI meeting transcription — local Whisper, no cloud
AI meeting summarizer — action items, questions, key points
Recording with NVENC hardware encoding to MP4
9 processing modes from 48fps Killer to CPU fallback
5 built-in profiles: Meeting, Streaming, Presentation, Gaming, Clean
Works with Zoom, Chrome, OBS, Discord, FaceTime

description.p: Live edge quality and microphone reliability patch

ul.li: Improved background replace around hair edges, finger gaps, and hands near the body during motion
Reduced beautify and relighting spill into head hair so hair looks more natural and less washed out
Kept the exported nvbroadcast microphone available even when voice effects and noise removal are turned off
Rechecked audio, video, meeting transcription, summaries, and packaging paths before release

description.p: Live background performance and stability patch

ul.li: Fixed the live background alpha path so one dedicated worker owns CUDA inference instead of many short-lived threads
Stopped repeated invalid-resource-handle failures and RVM state-reset loops that could make replace mode extremely laggy
Relighting now reuses the same-frame final matte, and replace-mode live processing caches more work instead of rebuilding it twice
Beautify keeps more GPU work inside the face ROI and preserves raw denoise history more carefully to reduce motion smear around the face and glasses

description.p: Stability and live-quality patch for meetings and streaming

ul.li: Stabilized effect and mode switching so the camera path is less likely to freeze, die, or hit device-busy teardown races
Fixed TensorRT detection for current tensorrt-cu12 package layouts and improved Zeus/Killer runtime handoff
Cleaned duplicate nvbroadcast mic and speaker entries and reduced startup audio restore churn
Scoped beautify denoise to the face ROI to reduce motion smear on the face and glasses
Reduced false replace-mode shoulder and underarm background breakout during raised-hand overlap

description.p: Audio and packaging reliability patch for meetings

ul.li: Stabilized the processed nvbroadcast microphone so browser and meeting apps can open it reliably on Linux
Fixed optional Whisper runtime validation and included the missing httpx dependency in the in-app installer path
Release installers now package the local meeting transcription runtime more consistently
Saved meeting finalization now reads the app's WAV capture directly instead of depending on an external ffmpeg binary
Release smoke and packaging CI now cover the meeting runtime path and are opted into Node 24

description.p: Meeting-first live quality and packaging update

ul.li: Reduced visible live delay by preferring fresh video frames and dropping stale buffers under load
Added persistent Auto - Adaptive tuning with weak-device warnings and capture-mode recommendations
Made face relighting brighten-biased and softened eye-contact behavior for more natural live meetings
Exposed the processed microphone as nvbroadcast and fixed speaker denoise to honor the selected output device
Improved update surfacing for GitHub releases, Snap installs, and macOS package downloads
Improved final meeting transcript quality with better chunk cleanup and full-audio finalization
Moved meeting transcript, notes, and summary finalization off the UI thread to avoid end-meeting hangs
Persisted speaker selection and active profile state across restarts
Added reset-to-defaults for returning to a known-good setup quickly
Fixed microphone test recording and playback and expanded test duration choices to 30s, 45s, and 60s
Stabilized the Linux virtual-camera sink path on v4l2loopback devices
Reduced live video lag by reusing face landmarks and limiting relighting to the face ROI
Tightened replace-mode edges around shoulders, hair, ears, and under-arm gaps
Improved local meeting transcription startup, chunk size, and saved meeting audio reliability
Resolution changes now save safely without hanging the live stream and apply after restart

description.p: Meeting assistant and guided runtime-install update

ul.li: New collapsible meeting sidebar with live local transcript and summary
Meeting history now stays on-device for 7 days and opens from the app
Meeting capture now records both-way audio for better notes and transcripts
Optional runtimes install in the background from the app with progress tracking
First-run setup now explains modes, skip flow, and required downloads more clearly
Tighter replace and remove mattes with better edge stability
Improved premium mode restore and startup consistency
Packaged CSS and desktop assets now ship correctly in wheels
Standard installer now performs a regular install instead of editable mode
Added release smoke checks for compile, tests, and wheel asset validation
AI meeting transcription (local Whisper, GPU-accelerated)
AI meeting summarizer (action items, key points)
Voice studio with 6 presets and GPU acceleration
Microphone and speaker selection
Audio VU meter and mic test
Recording with audio (NVENC + AAC)

description.p: Eye contact, relighting, Apple-like UI, profiles, macOS support
9 processing modes, edge refinement, video enhancement, system tray
